decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a positive live-LM PKV result; any future work should validate the same policy on external long-context benchmarks rather than another generic successor branch.
decision.stop_reason: The strengthened loss-magnitude shadow estimator recovered live-label recall on cached Qwen2.5-0.5B and met the success criterion with 30% fewer resident KV bytes than landmark@50 while preserving/improving logprob and recall.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a positive live-LM PKV result; any future work should validate the same policy on external long-context benchmarks rather than another generic successor branch.
run_notes_tail: ## Recommended next step

Continue in the same project by strengthening the live-LM policy signal: train/evaluate on more examples with a less toy local model (for example Qwen2.5-0.5B or OPT-125M), add loss-magnitude regression/ranking instead of binary labels, and tune features so shadow preserves live-label recall while retaining the observed logprob/footprint gains.
## 2026-04-22 stronger live-LM policy refinement plan

- Strengthen `src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py` without changing the base LM: add non-leaky block/query features, train the shadow scorer on loss magnitude/rank as well as binary live labels, and expose objective knobs for calibration.
- Run a compile/smoke pass first, then a short throughput calibration on cached `Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct` (larger than SmolLM2-135M) before the calibrated benchmark.

- Compare shadow vs landmark at 50% resident KV and at lower resident budgets on logprob preservation, live-label recall, restored KV bytes, latency, scored tokens/sec, RSS, CUDA/UMA telemetry, and decide whether the same mechanism still deserves continuation.
## 2026-04-22 stronger loss-magnitude live-LM results

- Updated `src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py` with stronger non-leaky block/query features (`inv_position`, `near_landmark`, rare/numeric query overlap, marker strength, block length) and a configurable shadow training objective (`--training-objective binary|magnitude|hybrid`, `--positive-weight`, `--magnitude-weight`). The hybrid objective keeps recall pressure on all live-relevant blocks while ranking high answer-logprob-loss blocks higher.

- Compile/smoke verification passed: `python3 -m py_compile src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py` and cached tiny GPT-2 smoke wrote `artifacts/live_lm_hybrid_smoke/summary.json`; `/usr/bin/time -v` logged 2.72s wall and 1,648,204 KB RSS in `artifacts/live_lm_hybrid_smoke.log`.

- Short throughput calibration on cached `Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct` passed: `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_calibration/summary.json`; internal elapsed 2.63s, scored-token throughput 1968.4 tok/s, `/usr/bin/time -v` wall 5.95s, max RSS 2,796,156 KB.

- Calibrated run: `.venv/bin/python src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --train-examples 28 --eval-examples 12 --blocks 20 --max-block-tokens 24 --budgets 0.25,0.35,0.5,0.65,0.75 --device auto --training-objective hybrid --epochs 36 --positive-weight 3.5 --magnitude-weight 0.7 --out-dir artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main`.

- Runtime/perf evidence from `/usr/bin/time -v`: 38.03s wall, 104% CPU, max RSS 2,794,140 KB. Script telemetry: elapsed 35.30s, approx scored-token throughput 3080.3 tok/s, CUDA available on GB10. UMA/GPU snapshot recorded in `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_system_snapshot.txt`.

- Same-budget result at 50% KV: shadow vs landmark improved logprob preservation by 2.348 points (0.98622 vs 0.96274), improved live-label recall by 21.619 points (0.625341 vs 0.40915), reduced answer-logprob drop by 1.288495, reduced restored-KV bytes by 9.615%, and reduced p95 total latency by 0.804% at equal resident KV.

- Lower-budget success criterion: shadow@35 matched/exceeded landmark@50 on logprob preservation and live-label recall while using 30.0% fewer resident KV bytes. Shadow@35 summary: logprob_preservation=0.98343, live_label_recall=0.507669, resident_kv_bytes=2064384, restored_kv_bytes=1523712, p95_total_latency_ms=32.242503.

- Verification: installed `pytest` into the project venv after it was missing, then ran `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests`; there are currently no collected tests. `python3 -m py_compile src/*.py` passed.

- Interpretation: the stronger loss-magnitude/live-feature shadow policy recovered live-label recall and preserved the prior logprob/KV benefits on a larger cached causal LM. This supports the core wrapper/no-base-weight-update mechanism for this research branch, with the remaining caveat that prompts are deterministic synthetic retrieval/code/chat-style traces rather than an external long-context benchmark suite.
## Durable artifacts from stronger live-LM refinement
- `src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py`
- `artifacts/live_lm_hybrid_smoke/summary.json`, `artifacts/live_lm_hybrid_smoke.log`
- `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_calibration/summary.json`, `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_calibration.log`

- `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main/summary.json`, `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main/summary.csv`, `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main/raw_rows.jsonl`, `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main/block_labels.jsonl`, `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_main.log`
- `artifacts/live_lm_qwen_hybrid_system_snapshot.txt`, `artifacts/pytest_after_hybrid.log`, `artifacts/pytest_install.log`
## Recommended conclusion

Finalize this branch as positive/moderately supported: on live model-output PKV telemetry with cached Qwen2.5-0.5B, the strengthened shadow estimator beats simple landmark pinning at equal resident KV and meets the >=30% resident-KV reduction success criterion at no observed logprob/recall loss versus landmark@50.
## Recommended next step

Continue in the same project by strengthening the live-LM policy signal: train/evaluate on more examples with a less toy local model (for example Qwen2.5-0.5B or OPT-125M), add loss-magnitude regression/ranking instead of binary labels, and tune features so shadow preserves live-label recall while retaining the observed logprob/footprint gains.
## 2026-04-22 stronger live-LM policy refinement plan

- Strengthen `src/live_lm_shadow_kv_experiment.py` without changing the base LM: add non-leaky block/query features, train the shadow scorer on loss magnitude/rank as well as binary live labels, and expose objective knobs for calibration.
- Run a compile/smoke pass first, then a short throughput calibration on cached `Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct` (larger than SmolLM2-135M) before the calibrated benchmark.

- Compare shadow vs landmark at 50% resident KV and at lower resident budgets on logprob preservation, live-label recall, restored KV bytes, latency, scored tokens/sec, RSS, CUDA/UMA telemetry, and decide whether the same mechanism still deserves continuation.
